title: Querying for object in JSON field always returns NULL
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Result for querying for a path in json column is `null` in all rows when that path is an object in some rows and not set in others.
```
> select json.a, json.^a, json.a.b, json.c from test;
┌─json.a─┬─json.^`a`─────────────────────┬─json.a.b─┬─json.c──┐
1. │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ   │ {"b":"42","g":42.42}          │ 42       │ [1,2,3] │
2. │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ   │ {}                            │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ     │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ    │
3. │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ   │ {"b":"43","e":"10","g":43.43} │ 43       │ [4,5,6] │
└────────┴───────────────────────────────┴──────────┴─────────┘
```
I found that I could query `json.^a` to get almost what I want, but that is inefficient according to docs.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.9.2.1
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE test (json JSON) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test VALUES ('{"a" : {"b" : 42, "g" : 42.42}, "c" : [1, 2, 3], "d" : "2020-01-01"}'), ('{"f" : "Hello, World!", "d" : "2020-01-02"}'), ('{"a" : {"b" : 43, "e" : 10, "g" : 43.43}, "c" : [4, 5, 6]}');
```
### Expected behavior
```
> select json.a, json.^a, json.a.b, json.c from test;
┌─json.a────────────────────────┬─json.^`a`─────────────────────┬─json.a.b─┬─json.c──┐
1. │ {"b":"42","g":42.42}          │ {"b":"42","g":42.42}          │ 42       │ [1,2,3] │
2. │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ                          │ {}                            │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ     │ ᴺᵁᴸᴸ    │
3. │ {"b":"43","e":"10","g":43.43} │ {"b":"43","e":"10","g":43.43} │ 43       │ [4,5,6] │
└───────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────┴──────────┴─────────┘
```
I would expect that `json.a` would have objects in row 1 and 3 and `null` in 2, so it would work the same as when the queried field is a number (`json.a.b`) or an array (`json.c`).
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: SELECT json_extract(json, '$.a') AS json_a, json_extract(json, '$.a') AS json_a_path, json_extract(json, '$.a.b') AS json_a_b, json_extract(json, '$.c') AS json_c FROM test_json;
key_bug_features: Querying JSON field with mixed object paths returns NULL
